item.title: Sa’ar urges Fiji protesters to focus on positive activities while Ali criticises deployment of Police to monitor protest
'Lavalani Fiji' Su Samuels walks the runway with daughters in heartfelt Fashion Week moment
item.description: A heartfelt mother-daughter moment stole the spotlight at the Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week on Saturday as fashion designer Su Samuels walked the runway hand-in-hand with her two daughters after showcasing her latest collection. The owner of Lavalani Fiji became emotional as her daughters joined her on the catwalk, turning the conclusion of her showcase into a celebration of family, perseverance and
item.content:encoded:
A heartfelt mother-daughter moment stole the spotlight at the Vodafone Fiji Fashion Week on Saturday as fashion designer Su Samuels walked the runway hand-in-hand with her two daughters after showcasing her latest collection.

The owner of Lavalani Fiji became emotional as her daughters joined her on the catwalk, turning the conclusion of her showcase into a celebration of family, perseverance and the pursuit of dreams.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Samuels says one of the most memorable moments of the evening came when her daughters reached for her hand and walked beside her.

Insert: Su - Proud of my daughters June 01

The touching runway moment culminated with Samuels bowing in gratitude as the models completed their walk, creating a powerful display of mother-daughter unity that resonated with the audience.

Reflecting on her journey, Samuels acknowledged that building a business in Fiji had not been without its challenges.

She says she did face a lot of hurdles, but the key is to stay at it, enjoy what you do and know that there's always light at the end of the tunnel.

S amuels says she believes every obstacle presents an opportunity to learn and grow.

Samuels also took pride in seeing her signature masi-inspired prints transformed into wearable designs on the runway.

She revealed that several of the models showcasing her collection were walking in a fashion show for the first time.

Samuels says she hopes her story will inspire others, particularly women and mothers, to pursue their ambitions regardless of life's circumstances.

She challenged the notion that motherhood limits a woman's potential, saying many women underestimate what they can accomplish after starting a family.

Offering advice to aspiring entrepreneurs, Samuels encouraged people to take the first step rather than wait for perfect conditions.

Her journey from aspiring designer to fashion entrepreneur, celebrated alongside her daughters on the runway, served as a reminder that determination, family support and belief in one's vision can turn dreams into reality.

item.title: Higher fuel costs making life harder for families in Kerebula Nadi
item.description: Rising fuel prices are placing increasing pressure on family budgets, with one Nadi resident saying the latest increases are making it harder for working families to make ends meet. Arvin Sharma of Kerebula Settlement says the higher cost of essential items is having a significant impact on his family's finances, particularly as he is the sole breadwinner supporting his parents.
item.content:encoded:
Rising fuel prices are placing increasing pressure on family budgets, with one Nadi resident saying the latest increases are making it harder for working families to make ends meet.

Arvin Sharma of Kerebula Settlement says the higher cost of essential items is having a significant impact on his family's finances, particularly as he is the sole breadwinner supporting his parents.

Sharma says the increase in gas prices has been especially challenging, noting that a 12-kilogram gas cylinder now costs close to two days' wages for many workers.

He says after deductions and household expenses, there is very little left to save at the end of the month.

According to Sharma, the rising cost of fuel, groceries and other essentials has made it increasingly difficult to manage daily expenses, including transport and food costs.

He says his family has considered alternatives such as using firewood for cooking, but this is not an option because his mother suffers from asthma and would be affected by the smoke.

Instead, they are exploring the use of kerosene stoves as a more affordable alternative.

While Sharma says he is fortunate to have stable employment, he is concerned about the impact of rising living costs on low-income earners and daily wage workers.

He says many families in the same community rely on daily wages, making it difficult to cope with increasing fuel and household costs.

Sharma believes the burden is particularly heavy on workers who depend on daily earnings to provide food and other necessities for their families.

item.title: Nemani urges overseas workers to seek help before disengaging
Public Health Bill must reflect rural realities - Divisional Commissioner Navuku
item.description: Efforts to modernise Fiji's 90-year-old public health legislation should be closely aligned with disaster management plans, rural

item.title: 41-year-old Ra farmer aims to boost productivity with new tractor
item.description: 41-year-old Eparama Nasomia of Dama Village received a tractor and disc harrow through government assistance aimed at supporting farmers and improving agricultural productivity. The Ra farmer, who once relied on hired machinery and even cows to prepare his land, says a new tractor will significantly improve his farming operations and help increase his family's income.
item.content:encoded:
41-year-old Eparama Nasomia of Dama Village received a tractor and disc harrow through government assistance aimed at supporting farmers and improving agricultural productivity.

The Ra farmer, who once relied on hired machinery and even cows to prepare his land, says a new tractor will significantly improve his farming operations and help increase his family's income.

Nasomia, a former mechanic who has been farming for more than eight years, says preparing land was often expensive and time-consuming, affecting the efficiency of his work.

He says the new tractor will allow him to prepare land faster, work more efficiently and accomplish more on the farm.

Nasomia says the assistance will not only improve production but also help secure a better future for his family.

The Ministry of Agriculture says the provision of modern farming equipment is part of efforts to help farmers increase production, reduce operational challenges and strengthen livelihoods in rural communities.

The Ministry adds that investing in farm machinery is expected to contribute to the long-term growth and sustainability of Fiji's agricultural sector.
